summary.headline: THRESHOLD / EXTREME
summary.description: Score holds at 94 / THRESHOLD / EXTREME — no upward revision this cycle, but no de-escalation signals either. Key drivers: Iran missile strike toward Eilat (no casualties), IDF 7th Armored Brigade expanding Lebanon ground raids, IRGC evacuation warnings issued to US industrial sites across the Gulf region, and GDELT confirming drone attacks shutting a key Middle Eastern oil port. Laylatul Qadr window opens March 16 — 48-hour theological trigger period now imminent. No negotiation track visible.

alert_tape: SCORE 94 / THRESHOLD / EXTREME — 0 SINCE LAST UPDATE — LIVE
changelog: Iran fires missile toward Eilat — intercepted or missed, no casualties, but southern Israel targeting continues.
IDF 7th Armored Brigade kills dozens of Hezbollah operatives in Lebanon raids — ground operation expansion signaled.
IRGC issues evacuation warnings to residents near US industrial sites across the Gulf — pre-strike signaling pattern.
GDELT confirms drone attacks shutting key Middle Eastern oil port — Hormuz pressure extending to port infrastructure.
GDELT: ships reportedly sailing again through Hormuz per one source — contested picture, selective blockade enforcement uneven.
Score holds at 94 — no new threshold breach this cycle, but Laylatul Qadr window activates March 16.

vectors.name: Military Escalation
vectors.driver: Eilat missile + IDF Lebanon ground raids expanding
vectors.note: Score holds. New Eilat missile strike (no casualties) and IDF 7th Armored Brigade Lebanon raids confirm sustained multi-front kinetic tempo. IRGC evacuation warnings to Gulf industrial sites are a pre-strike signaling pattern — watch for follow-on strikes on US commercial infrastructure within 24-48 hours.

vectors.name: Geopolitical Stability
vectors.driver: French Lebanon proposal circulating, no traction
vectors.note: Score holds. French Israel-Lebanon proposal (including Israeli recognition) is circulating but has no visible acceptance path. Trump-Zelensky friction reported by TASS — treat as narrative framing, but the underlying tension over Ukraine deal-making is corroborated by Western sources. No negotiation window on Iran track.

vectors.name: Financial / Economic
vectors.driver: WTI $98.71 flat / GDELT port closure confirmed
vectors.note: WTI holds at $98.71 — no further surge this cycle but no retreat either. GDELT confirms drone attack shutting a key Gulf oil port, adding infrastructure disruption pressure beyond Kharg Island. Gold up 0.18% to $5,061.70. VIX flat at 27.19 — markets not yet pricing worst-case Kharg full disruption or Fifth Fleet engagement. Score holds pending next kinetic confirmation.

vectors.name: Theological Convergence
vectors.driver: Netanyahu Messiah statement + Paula White axis
vectors.note: All four traditions — Evangelical Christian, Messianic Jewish, Islamic, Orthodox Christian — pointing at the same geography with the same intensity simultaneously. No historical precedent for this convergence.

vectors.name: Third Temple / Al-Aqsa
vectors.note: Hegseth 2018 Jerusalem speech calling Third Temple reconstruction a miracle. IDF soldiers wearing Temple insignia. Ben-Gvir and Smotrich in cabinet with explicit Temple Mount agenda. Red heifers in Israel. Temple vessels built. Architectural plans drawn.

vectors.name: Information Warfare
vectors.driver: FCC license threats confirmed / prediction market crackdown calls
vectors.note: Score holds. FCC broadcaster license threat confirmed across NYT, MEE, and Investing.com this cycle. New signal: BBC reports calls to crack down on prediction markets hosting 'gruesome' war bets — regulatory pressure expanding from broadcast to financial speculation on war outcomes. Information warfare vector now operating on broadcast, social media arrest, AI-targeting, and financial market tracks simultaneously.

intel_cards.title: Iran fires missile toward Eilat — no casualties, southern Israel targeting continues
intel_cards.summary: Iran launched a missile toward Eilat, Israel's southern Red Sea port city. Magen David Adom and United Hatzalah confirmed no hits or injuries. Missile intercepted or missed. Eilat targeting is strategically significant — it is Israel's only Red Sea port and a key economic and tourism hub, separate from the northern front.
intel_cards.analyst_note: Eilat targeting signals Iran is probing Israel's southern defensive perimeter — a different intercept layer than the north. No casualties this strike, but each Eilat attempt depletes interceptor stocks that are already critically low. If Iran calibrates a saturation strike toward Eilat while simultaneously barraging the north, the intercept math becomes untenable. Watch for Eilat strike frequency as a leading indicator of Iran's intercept-depletion strategy.

intel_cards.title: IDF 7th Armored Brigade expands Lebanon raids — dozens of Hezbollah operatives killed, ground operation signaled
intel_cards.summary: Israel's 7th Armored Brigade conducted raids in southern Lebanon killing dozens of Hezbollah operatives, seizing weapons, and dismantling infrastructure. IDF is actively weighing broader ground operations in southern Lebanon. BBC ground reporting confirms a family killed in a separate Israeli strike — IDF says it targeted terrorist infrastructure, family says civilians.
intel_cards.analyst_note: 7th Armored Brigade deployment into Lebanon is a significant escalation indicator — this is a heavy armor formation, not a special forces raid unit. Armored brigade activity in southern Lebanon signals Israel is preparing or executing a ground maneuver campaign, not just air and artillery strikes. If a full ground operation is authorized, Lebanon becomes a third active ground front alongside Gaza and the Iran air campaign. Lebanon death toll already at 826 including 106 children.

intel_cards.title: IRGC issues evacuation warnings to residents near US industrial sites across the Gulf
intel_cards.summary: Iran's IRGC issued warnings to residents living near US industrial sites across the Middle East to evacuate, and warned all US businesses in the region to leave. This is a pre-strike signaling pattern — IRGC has used similar warnings before kinetic action against specific target sets. Source is TASS; treat as ASSESSMENT pending corroboration.
intel_cards.analyst_note: NARRATIVE MONITOR — TASS is Russian state media; this report serves Russian framing interests by amplifying Iranian threat posture. However, the IRGC evacuation warning pattern is operationally significant regardless of source: Iran used similar pre-strike warnings before the January 2020 Ain al-Assad strike and before the April 2024 drone barrage. If this warning is genuine, it signals imminent IRGC strikes on US commercial and industrial infrastructure across the Gulf — a target set expansion beyond military bases. Watch for corroboration from MEE, Al Jazeera, or Reuters within 6 hours.

intel_cards.title: GDELT: Drone attack shuts key Middle Eastern oil port — Hormuz pressure extending to port infrastructure
intel_cards.summary: GDELT conflict monitor (Express.co.uk) reports a drone attack has shut a key Middle Eastern oil port. Separate GDELT signals confirm drone attacks in Kuwait and ongoing Hormuz interdiction pressure. One GDELT source (HotAir) reports ships are again sailing through Hormuz — suggesting enforcement of the selective blockade is uneven or contested. Port closure compounds Kharg Island strike damage to Iran's export infrastructure.
intel_cards.analyst_note: Port closure via drone attack is a different attack vector than missile strikes on Kharg Island — it suggests Iran or its proxies are using drone swarms to interdict Gulf port operations directly, not just threatening Hormuz transit. If the closed port is a UAE or Kuwaiti facility, this expands the conflict footprint to Gulf state commercial infrastructure. The contested Hormuz picture — one source says ships are sailing, others confirm interdiction — suggests Iran is selectively enforcing the blockade rather than imposing a blanket closure, consistent with Araghchi's legal framing.

intel_cards.title: Trump calls for allied warships at Hormuz — UK, Gulf states, Asian importers targeted for coalition
intel_cards.summary: Trump publicly called on allied nations — specifically naming the UK and nations dependent on Gulf oil — to send warships to secure the Strait of Hormuz. GDELT confirms Trump urged the UK directly. gCaptain confirms the call for allied warships. Iran's selective blockade framing (open to all except US/Israel) is designed to fracture this coalition before it forms.
intel_cards.analyst_note: Coalition formation at Hormuz is the critical variable for the next 72 hours. If UK, France, or Asian importers decline to join — accepting Iran's framing that their ships are not threatened — the US is left conducting unilateral escort operations, which Iran can frame as aggression. GDELT also flags a report that Iran's Hormuz reopening condition 'hands China a major financial win' — suggesting Beijing may be negotiating a side deal to keep its tankers flowing while the US-Iran standoff continues. Watch for Chinese diplomatic signaling on Hormuz in the next 24 hours.

intel_cards.title: France proposes Israel-Lebanon peace deal including Lebanese recognition of Israel
intel_cards.summary: A French proposal for an Israel-Lebanon peace agreement is circulating, reportedly including Lebanese recognition of Israel, Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon, and a path to normalized relations. No acceptance signals from either party. The proposal is the only visible diplomatic track in the current cycle.
intel_cards.analyst_note: The French proposal is diplomatically significant but operationally irrelevant at current escalation tempo. Lebanon recognition of Israel is a maximalist Israeli demand that has no domestic political support in Beirut under current conditions. The proposal's existence signals European anxiety about conflict expansion but does not constitute a credible off-ramp. If Israel is weighing a full ground operation in southern Lebanon, a French paper proposal will not deter it. File as WATCH — not an active de-escalation signal.

intel_cards.title: Israel-US regime change operation active — psychological ops targeting Iranian population
intel_cards.summary: Jerusalem Post confirms Israel and the US are actively increasing efforts to encourage Iranian protesters to take to the streets and overthrow the regime. An Israeli official stated regime change is 'more likely after the war, during a time of relative calm' — suggesting the current kinetic campaign is not expected to produce immediate regime collapse. Simultaneous strikes on civilian-industrial targets (Isfahan factory, schools) contradict the psychological operation's stated goal.
intel_cards.analyst_note: The Israeli official's admission that regime change is a post-war goal — not a current-war outcome — is a significant strategic signal. It means Israel and the US are not expecting the kinetic campaign to produce regime collapse, and are planning for a sustained post-war pressure phase. This extends the conflict timeline beyond the current kinetic exchange. Meanwhile, strikes on Isfahan factories and schools are hardening Iranian nationalist sentiment — the psyop and the strike campaign are working against each other. This strategic contradiction is a planning failure with escalation consequences.

intel_cards.title: Prediction markets hosting millions in war bets — calls for regulatory crackdown
intel_cards.summary: BBC reports prediction markets have hosted millions of dollars in bets on war outcomes, including 'gruesome' contracts on specific events. Calls for regulatory crackdown are growing. This is a new information warfare and financial market integrity signal — war outcome speculation is now a mainstream financial product.
intel_cards.analyst_note: Prediction market war bets create perverse incentive structures — large positions on specific escalation events (leader assassination, nuclear use, port closure) create financial motivation to amplify or fabricate triggering information. The Netanyahu assassination disinfo that required an official denial this cycle is consistent with adversarial actors attempting to move prediction market positions. Regulatory crackdown on these markets, if it comes, will itself become an information warfare battleground. Flag for Information Warfare vector.

intel_cards.title: U.S. air tanker crash in Iraq — GDELT flags KC-135 loss parallel
intel_cards.summary: GDELT flags a report (timesca.com) of a US air tanker crash in Iraq, drawing comparison to the KC-135 loss in Kyrgyzstan in 2013. Details are sparse. If confirmed, a tanker loss in Iraq during active hostilities — with US Embassy Baghdad already struck twice — would represent a significant US asset loss in the Iraqi theater.
intel_cards.analyst_note: LOW confidence — single GDELT source, no corroboration from major outlets yet. However, a tanker aircraft loss in Iraq during active hostilities is a significant escalation signal if confirmed: air tankers are force multipliers for strike packages, and their loss degrades US offensive reach. Cross-reference against DoD statements and major wire services. If confirmed, Military Escalation vector warrants upward revision.

intel_cards.title: Global anti-war protests: Madrid thousands, Seoul hundreds — public pressure building
intel_cards.summary: Thousands rallied in Madrid warning the Iran war may spiral into a wider conflict. Hundreds protested in Seoul against US-Israeli strikes. Both protests occurred on the same day — Day 13 of the war. Global public opposition is building across non-aligned and allied nations simultaneously.
intel_cards.analyst_note: Simultaneous protests in European capitals and Asian cities on Day 13 signal that allied-nation domestic political pressure on governments to distance from US-Israeli operations is building. Spain and South Korea are NATO-adjacent or treaty-allied states — their populations protesting creates political constraints on those governments joining Trump's Hormuz coalition. Watch for European government statements distancing from US operations in the next 48-72 hours as domestic pressure mounts ahead of Laylatul Qadr.

intel_cards.title: IRGC claims direct strikes on US military bases in Bahrain and Kuwait — Fifth Fleet targeted
intel_cards.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards claim missile and drone attacks on US military bases in Bahrain and Kuwait, including facilities linked to the US Navy's Fifth Fleet. Statement published via Sepah News. US confirmation pending. If verified, this marks the first direct IRGC strike on Fifth Fleet infrastructure — a significant escalation beyond proxy attacks.
intel_cards.analyst_note: IRGC claim via Sepah News — treat as ASSESSMENT until US DoD confirms or denies. If verified, this is the single most significant escalation in the current cycle: direct state-on-state kinetic action against the US Navy's regional command hub. Fifth Fleet at Manama is the operational backbone of Hormuz enforcement. Striking it signals Iran is willing to absorb the escalation cost of hitting US command infrastructure directly, not just through proxies. Watch for US retaliatory strike authorization within 12-24 hours.

intel_cards.title: Iran FM Araghchi: Hormuz open to all except US and Israeli vessels — selective blockade declared
intel_cards.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi publicly declared the Strait of Hormuz open to all nations except the United States and Israel. Other vessels are free to pass, though many are avoiding the strait due to security concerns Araghchi attributed to US-Israeli operations. This reframes the Hormuz closure from a blanket blockade to a targeted interdiction — with significant legal and coalition implications.
intel_cards.analyst_note: This is a deliberate legal and diplomatic maneuver. By framing the closure as targeted at US/Israeli vessels only, Iran attempts to fracture the allied coalition Trump is assembling — China, India, and non-aligned states have no formal obligation to join an escort mission if Iran claims their ships are not threatened. Watch for whether Gulf states and Asian importers accept this framing. If they do, the US-led naval coalition loses its multilateral justification. This is information warfare and legal warfare operating simultaneously with kinetic operations.

doctrine.title: Netanyahu Messiah Doctrine — Fully Documented
doctrine.quote: "We will make it to the return of the Messiah, but this will not happen next Thursday."
doctrine.quote_source: — PRIME MINISTER BENJAMIN NETANYAHU // MARCH 12, 2026 // ACTIVE WARTIME STATEMENT

cards.text: The late Rebbe Schneerson told a young Netanyahu 30 years ago: "You will be the prime minister who passes the scepter to the Messiah." That prophecy is now circulating openly in Israeli media during active war.

Netanyahu framed explicitly as an accelerationist — someone who could precipitate or force God's intervention in history. Kohanim (Temple priests) being actively recruited by his government for Temple service.
Netanyahu on record to i24: "If you're asking if I have a sense of mission, historically and spiritually, the answer is yes." Accepted amulet of Greater Israel map saying "This is my vision."

overlay.description: The Night of Decree — when divine destinies are written — falls this week inside an active regional war centered on Al-Quds and Al-Aqsa. The theological significance within the Islamic eschatological framework cannot be overstated. No Western analytical model is tracking this variable.

cards.text: ADL published comprehensive bias report March 2025 explicitly lobbying to reshape AI responses on Israel. Claude, GPT, Gemini, Llama all targeted. 34,400 queries submitted. Calling on AI companies to change training data.

ADL CEO Greenblatt testified before Israeli Knesset January 7, 2025 — American organization reporting to foreign parliament. Invoked Mossad pager operation as the model for information warfare strategy.
ADL education programs documented across K-12 curriculum. Wikipedia editors banned after ADL pressure. Social media declared 8th front of organized information campaign. All operating simultaneously.

triggers.text: Iran bank-targeting declaration executed — first Gulf financial institution physically struck.
Hormuz mining confirmed operational — tanker sunk or disabled, full closure triggered.
Al-Aqsa physically threatened or accessed by Israeli nationalists during active war.
Russia moves from intelligence-sharing to active military involvement.
Iran deploys Fattah-2 hypersonic at scale — no proven intercept solution exists.
US Congress invokes War Powers — or fails to, triggering constitutional crisis.
